name: How to Choose the Right Local Listing Management Software for Your Business
description: Identify local listing management software for your multiple-location brand. Find key features, integrations, and quality tools to streamline your business
main_title: How to Choose the Right Local Listing Management Software
richtext:
Managing your brand’s online presence can be a complicated ordeal, especially with businesses in multiple locations. Local listing management software ensures that each location of your business has precise and reliable information across directories, social media platforms, and review sites.  

Whether your business manages 10 locations or 100, having the appropriate tools in place will save time, enhance local search performance, and boost customer trust. Local listing management software allows businesses to automate and unify the control of their listings, ensuring they remain visible, up-to-date, and dependable across all platforms.

Key Features to Look for in Local Listing Management Software

When deciding on the right software, it’s essential to know which features are important for your business. Below is a rundown of the features to prioritize:

  1. A Unified Dashboard

The core of any effective business listing management software is a unified or centralized dashboard. This feature allows you to control all business locations from a single interface, enabling efficient management without the need to log in to separate platforms for each listing.  

Having a centralized view allows you to make updates—such as changing business hours or promoting offers—across all locations simultaneously. Additionally, the unified dashboard helps track performance across platforms, making it easier to identify areas where the listings need improvement.

  1. Automated Listing Updates

Automated listing updates save an incredible amount of time. Instead of manually updating each listing across various platforms, the API-based software ensures that changes are applied in real-time across directories like Google My Business, Yelp, Facebook, and more. This is essential for maintaining consistent information, which helps prevent confusion among customers and enhances the integrity of your brand.

  1. Review & Reputation Management

The online reputation of your business is an invaluable asset, and reviews assist in creating it. An effective local listing tool tracks reviews and allows you to react to them immediately. Managing reviews across platforms from a single interface saves an incredible amount of time and allows you to maintain a consistent brand voice. Managing reviews could involve thanking a customer for a positive review or responding to a negative one.

Additionally, there are other tools that provide solicitation features, encouraging happy customers to leave positive feedback, which is important when increasing your business's status and reputation.

  1. Duplicate Listing Management

Duplicate listings can negatively affect your SEO performance and create confusion for prospective clients. Search engines, like Google, may penalize your website if they detect conflicting information across multiple directories. A robust local listing management software helps identify and eliminate duplicate listings, protecting your online presence.  

In addition to enhancing SEO, managing duplicates ensures that clients find the correct information with just a click, reducing frustration caused by contradictory data.

  1. Analytics & Reporting

Your business listing management software should provide detailed analytics and reporting features to assist in tracking your listings’ implementation. Data is extremely important in the modern digital landscape. Visibility, search rankings, local profile metrics, and review trends are important metrics for enhancing your local SEO tools.

To report consistently doesn’t only help monitor your recent performance but also allows you to make data-driven assessments.

  1. Pricing

With local listing tools, there needs to be a balance between affordability and functionality. The concern of pricing is always valid, especially for multiple-location businesses. Some of these tools offer a free trial or a demo period to test out the capabilities of the listing tool and ensure that it is worth investing in this software for your business. Synup works on a per location pricing which means you can invest money as you scale and only pay for the locations you are actively managing via our platform.

  1. Scalability

For a multiple-location business, the prospect of expanding is possible. So, it is important to think about the future and what that future would look like. The software you choose needs to adapt to the business’s growth without having to refurbish the system completely. When deciding on the local listing management software, check to see if it offers flexible plans to manage larger networks with ease.

Integrations with Other Tools

Existing tools like CRM (Customer Relationship Management), POS (Point of Sale) systems, and marketing automation platforms should integrate seamlessly with your local listing management software. Ensuring this integration guarantees that your existing data merges smoothly between systems, allowing you to make better-informed decisions.  

It's also crucial to check the business listing management software’s network of partner directories and review sites. Essentially, the more platforms the tool supports, the better visibility your business will have.

How Accessible is Customer Support?

Even the best software isn’t completely immune to technical issues. In many industries, customer support is crucial, and this holds true for multi-location businesses as well. Ideally, a local listing tool should offer round-the-clock support, comprehensive documentation, and even video tutorials.

Source: Freepik

Having an account manager or customer success manager assigned would be especially beneficial. These account managers can assist with onboarding, provide ongoing support, and ensure that your business is maximizing the value of the software.

Below are some of the best local listing management software tools at your disposal today:

  1. Synup
  • Renowned for its all-inclusive features and user-friendly interface, Synup provides programmed listing updates, review management, and detailed analytics.
  • Multiple-location businesses are what Synup is designed for by providing scalability and integration choices.
  • This platform is perfect for businesses that want to program their local SEO efforts while preserving control over their brand’s presence online.

Book a demo today to discover Synup’s incredible features.

  • Moz Local is another software management contender that helps businesses manage their listings, track reviews, and monitor local search performance.
  • The focus is on SEO, making it a quality choice for businesses that want to improve their local SEO tools.
  1. Yext:
  • The last, but definitely not the least, powerful local listing tool is renowned for its vigorous network of partner directories and sturdy review management features.
  •  Yext is an ideal option for larger enterprises with a massive network of locations.

Also Read: 17 Best Local Listings Management Tools

How to Make Your Final Decision

Now that you've narrowed down the local listing tools to meet your business needs, it’s time to make your final decision. To do this, follow these steps:

  1. Get Demos or Free Trials

Many software providers offer demos or free trials before deciding to ensure that their features work best for you and your business. This is your opportunity to see how effectively the local listing management software integrates with your existing tools and if it meets your business needs.

  1. Assess Customer Support

Once you decide which local listing management software to trial, closely evaluate its customer support team. This could give you a good idea of its services and how well it can assist you once you have committed to the platform.

  1. Consider Long-Term Costs

Sometimes, the initial costs can be daunting. However, by looking beyond them and considering the future, you can recognize the benefits of investing in local listing management software. Conversely, some tools may seem affordable upfront but could become expensive in the long run. It’s essential to ensure that the local listing tool can scale with your business growth without significantly increasing costs.
‍

Summing Up

For any multi-location business, choosing the right local listing management software is a crucial decision. The right tool can streamline operations, enhance your online presence, and strengthen your brand’s reputation. Overall, Synup stands out as an exceptional option for businesses seeking a feature-rich, scalable, and user-friendly platform.

Book a demo today to discover how Synup could improve and transform your local listing management strategy. 
‍

How to Choose the Right Local Listing Management Software for Your Business: FAQs

  1. What is local listing management software?

Local listing management software plays a crucial role by allowing businesses to manage their listings across numerous directories, ensuring consistent and accurate information across all platforms.

  1. Why is programmed/automated listing important?

These updates save a lot of time and ensure accuracy across all of your business locations, reducing the risk of errors that could obscure prospective clients.

  1. Which is the best local listing tool?

Synup is the top choice for multiple-location businesses and reseller partners. It is easy to use, has automation features, and has strong review management. Moz Local and Yext are also tools that offer good features for the required needs.

  1. How can I incorporate my CRM with a local listing tool?

Numerous local listing management software tools, such as Synup, offer CRM integrations, which permit you to sync customer data and simplify evaluation collection across platforms.
